GROUP 2 • Processed culinary ingredients
Oil, sugar, salt... These are ingredients we add for cooking, but that we don’t eat on their own.
GROUP 3 • Processed foods
Here, we find group 1 foods to which a little salt, sugar or oil has been added: bread, cheese, canned vegetables.
GROUP 4 • Ultra-processed foods. These are industrial products with lots of ingredients you don’t find in a typical kitchen (additives, artificial flavors…). Examples: sodas, chips, nuggets, candies.
MARKETING:
TRUE INFORMATION OR REAL MISINFORMATION?
Studies have shown that the more a food label is filled with information, the lower the quality of the product. In general, ultra-processed and less healthy foods use more aggressive marketing strategies and super busy labels.
“No added sugar” does not mean sugar-free (natural sugar is still there).
“Light”? If it’s reduced in one way, it’s because something has been added elsewhere to achieve a “similar” result. Example: foods reduced in fat often contain e
